name: k-essence dark energy
summary: Dark energy driven by a scalar field with non-canonical kinetic terms: L = p(phi, X) where X = (1/2)(d phi)^2. The non-trivial dependence on X allows attractor solutions in which the field tracks the dominant fluid (matter, radiation) and only emerges as dark energy near the matter-radiation transition. The 'why now' problem of dark-energy onset is addressed dynamically without anthropic input.

addresses_puzzles.note: Tracker behaviour explains why dark energy is comparable to matter density at the present epoch (the 'why now' problem) without fine-tuning of initial conditions. Doesn't address the underlying smallness of the late-time vacuum energy.
K-essence with non-canonical kinetic terms can exhibit attractor solutions for which DE/DM ratio is naturally O(1) at recent epochs (Armendariz-Picon-Mukhanov-Steinhardt 2000).
Non-canonical kinetic term allows w(z) crossing of -1 (phantom-like) which standard quintessence cannot do, fitting DESI 2024 data more easily.

touches_benchmarks.note: k-essence has speed of sound c_s != 1 in general, leading to distinct clustering signatures relative to LCDM and quintessence; constrained by RSD measurements.

introduces.note: Functional form of p(phi, X) (typically two-parameter family) plus initial conditions.

history: Armendariz-Picon, Damour, Mukhanov (1999) introduced k-inflation. Armendariz-Picon, Mukhanov, Steinhardt (2000), 'A dynamical solution to the problem of a small cosmological constant and late-time cosmic acceleration', PRL 85, 4438, arXiv:astro-ph/0004134 — applied to dark energy. Active phenomenology in growth-of-structure and ISW data through 2010s-2020s; DESI Y1 evolving-DE hint compatible with k-essence.

references.citation: Armendariz-Picon, Mukhanov, Steinhardt, 'A dynamical solution to the problem of a small cosmological constant and late-time cosmic acceleration', PRL 85, 4438 (2000)
